content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA                                    GEORGE DEUKMEJIAN, Governor

CALIFORNIA HORSE RACING BOARD
1010 HURLEY WAY, SUITE 190
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95825
(916) 920-7178


February 5, 1990


Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA  95814

Dear Ms. Donovan:

I would appreciate your providing me with a letter of advice
concerning whether it is necessary that I disqualify myself
from participation in making decisions as a member of the
California Horse Racing Board, based upon the following
information:

1.  General Information

    I am employed by Oland International Insurance Brokers,
an insurance brokerage firm based in Los Angeles with
branches in New York and Kansas City.  The firm employs
approximately 70 people, and the firm's annual revenues
are approximately $10 million.  Our clientele are solely
corporate business accounts (as opposed to personal
"homeowner" policies).  The operations are divided into
2 main sections: (1) Property and Casualty Insurance
(Workers' Compensation, Public Liability, Automobile,
Fire, etc.,) and (2) Employee Benefits (Group Medical,
Life, Travel Accident, Long Term Disability, Pension
Plans, etc.,).

    I have a title of Senior Vice President, and I own stock
which amounts to 1% of the company.  My day-to-day
activities are confined entirely to servicing property
and casualty insurances for 8 corporate clients, none of
which is involved with Horse Racing in any way.  These 8
accounts are involved in Oil production, restaurants,
property management, airplane propeller manufacturing,
and computer component manufacturing.  I am not involved
in solicitation of new business, and I am not involved
in employee benefit coverages.  I am a salaried employee
and I do not participate in any profit sharing programs.

Kathryn E. Donovan
February 5, 1990
Page 2


2.  Racing Associations

    The employee benefits section of our firm is presently
    involved in the following:

    a.  A person in our employee benefits department
        presently handles a small "Long Term Disability"
        insurance policy for a Racing association licensed
        by the CHRB.  The annual income to the firm is
        approximately $1,900.  I had no involvement in the
        acquisition of this account, I am not involved in
        the handling or decision making, and I derive no
        income from it.

    b.  A person in our Employee Benefits Department
        presently handles a group medical and dental
        insurance policy for another Racing association
        which is also licensed by the CHRB.  The annual
        income to the firm is approximately $10,500.  As
        with item (a) above, I was not involved in the
        acquisition of the account, I am not involved in
        the handling or decision making, and I derive no
        income from it.  I have been informed that our
        Employee Benefits department submitted a bid in
        competition with several other brokerage firms, and
        our bid was the best, and we were awarded the
        business on that basis.

3.  Unions which represent Horse Racing employees

    A person in our Employee Benefits department presently
    handles a Group Medical, Life and Dental insurance
    policy for the Health and Welfare Trust of a union which
    represents horse racing employees.  The annual income to
    the firm is approximately $45,000.  As with items 2 (a)
    and 2 (b) above, I was not involved in the acquisition
    of the account, I am not involved in the handling or
    decision making, and I derive no income from it.

4.  Individual Licensees

    An individual licensee (such as a horse owner) may at
    some future time ask our firm to handle the insurance
    for his own business which is unrelated to Horse Racing
    (such as a company which manufactures furniture).  This
    situation does not exist at the present time to my

Kathryn E. Donovan
February 5, 1990
Page 3

knowledge, but I would appreciate your including an
opinion, on the presumption that I would not personally
be involved in the acquisition, handling of the account
or decision making, and would derive no income from it.

Thanks for your assistance.

Very truly yours,

[Signature]

Leslie M. Liscom, Member
California Horse Racing Board

lml/23

cc: Leonard Foote, Executive Secretary, CHRB
    Henry Chavez, Chairman, CHRB
    Robert Mukai, Deputy Attorney General
